fields.description: Legacy boolean that selects the Salesforce login/token host when `tokenUrl` is empty (`true` → https://test.salesforce.com, `false` → https://login.salesforce.com). Deprecated in favor of `tokenUrl`; the config editor no longer writes it but reads it to derive the initial Environment selection, and the backend still honors it for backwards compatibility and provisioning.

relationships.description: Username-password OAuth credentials (authType='user'): the Salesforce username, password, security token, and the connected app's consumer key and secret used in the OAuth2 password grant.

JWT bearer credentials (authType='jwt'): the connected app's consumer key (JWT issuer), the Salesforce username (JWT subject), and the certificate and private key of the connected app's digital signature used to sign the JWT assertion.
Environment selection: the legacy sandbox boolean and the tokenUrl it falls back to when tokenUrl is empty.

instructions.msg: Two authentication methods are available, selected via jsonData.authType: 'user' (username-password OAuth, the default) and 'jwt' (OAuth JWT bearer flow). Configure exactly one method; only the credentials of the selected method are validated/used. When jsonData.authType is empty the backend defaults it to 'user' (pkg/models/settings.go:82-90).

To configure username-password auth, set jsonData.authType='user' and provide jsonData.user plus secureJsonData.password, secureJsonData.clientID (connected app consumer key) and secureJsonData.clientSecret (connected app consumer secret). Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authType":"user","tokenUrl":"https://login.salesforce.com"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password>","clientID":"<consumer-key>","clientSecret":"<consumer-secret>"}}. secureJsonData.securityToken is optional and, when set, is concatenated onto the password before the token request (pkg/plugin/client.go:104); user, password, clientID and clientSecret are all required (pkg/models/settings.go:112-123).

To configure JWT bearer auth, set jsonData.authType='jwt' and provide secureJsonData.cert and secureJsonData.privateKey (the connected app's digital-signature certificate and RSA private key). Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authType":"jwt","user":"<username>","tokenUrl":"https://login.salesforce.com"},"secureJsonData":{"clientID":"<consumer-key>","cert":"<PEM cert>","privateKey":"<PEM key>"}}. Only cert and privateKey are enforced by settings validation (pkg/models/settings.go:103-110), but jsonData.user (JWT subject) and secureJsonData.clientID (JWT issuer) are also required for the token request to succeed (pkg/plugin/client.go:96, pkg/jwt/jwt.go:57-64) — a JWT config missing either loads successfully but fails at connect time.

Legacy auth interpretation: datasources created before authType was mandatory store no jsonData.authType. The backend's normalizeAuthType (pkg/models/settings.go:82-90) can in principle infer 'jwt' from cert+privateKey being set and password empty, but it inspects the jsonData-level struct fields, which are empty because these secrets live in secureJsonData and are copied only after normalization runs — so in practice a missing authType always resolves to 'user'. The config editor does the real detection from secureJsonFields (cert && privateKey && !password => 'jwt', else 'user'; src/views/SFConfigEditor.tsx:29-37). Do not assume a missing authType means unconfigured.

secureJsonData values (password, securityToken, clientID, clientSecret, cert, privateKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.privateKey == true) instead of secureJsonData. Note the plugin stores clientID/clientSecret as secrets (secureJsonData), not jsonData.

jsonData.tokenUrl selects the Salesforce OAuth host: 'https://login.salesforce.com' (Production, the default) or 'https://test.salesforce.com' (SandBox). When tokenUrl is empty the backend derives it from the legacy jsonData.sandbox boolean (pkg/models/settings.go:92-100). The token endpoint is <tokenUrl>/services/oauth2/token (pkg/plugin/client.go:92); the actual data API base URL is NOT tokenUrl but the instance_url returned in the token response (pkg/plugin/client.go:143-144,368-379). The datasource's own root url field is unused.
